---
title: "scverse: a software infrastructure for bioinformatics and single-cell biology"
author: "Luca Marconato, Wolfgang Huber"
subtitle: "deRSE26 - Conference for Research Software Engineering in Germany"
date: "2026-03-03"
date-format: iso
format:
  revealjs: 
    theme: [default, wh.scss]
    transition: slide
    scrollable: true
    slide-number: c/t
    show-slide-number: all
    auto-stretch: false
    center-title-slide: false
    logo: EMBL_120x128.png         # may not good due to TUM - put an scverse logo instead
  html:
    code-line-numbers: false
    embed-resources: true
execute: 
  warning: true
  error: false
  message: false
slide-level: 1
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
engine: knitr            # only if we use living code with the presentation; I suppose not...
knitr:
  opts_chunk: 
    R.options:
      width: 120
---

# About scverse & EMBL

**scverse** is a community-driven ecosystem of Python packages for single-cell omics data analysis, providing standardized tools and best practices for biological research.

**EMBL** (European Molecular Biology Laboratory) is Europe's flagship laboratory for the life sciences, conducting research and providing training in molecular biology, with a strong focus on computational biology and bioinformatics.

Together, they support open-source software development and collaborative research in the life sciences.

# Slide 2

# Slide 3
